title: iris pumila subsp. grandis
intro: Iris pumila subsp. grandis, also known as the dwarf iris or Alpine iris, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the iris family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Iris pumila subsp. grandis include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
